title: Music, Visual and Performing Arts Bursaries South Africa 2026
description_short: The ARTS career field is one in which your success is strongly determined by your passion and drive. The salary ranges in this job sector are very vast – with the right skills, ambition and opportunit
description_full: The ARTS career field is one in which your success is strongly determined by your passion and drive. The salary ranges in this job sector are very vast – with the right skills, ambition and opportunities, you could go from being a “starving artist” to a world famous artist. If you love what you do (most people in the arts sector do), then success is sure to follow. Whether you dream of pursuing a career in visual arts (painting, drawing, photography, film making, video making, design, sculpture, ceramics and crafts) or performing arts (dance, drama and singing), passion is ultimately the key to your success. In order to qualify for a bursary within the ARTS sector, you will generally have to submit or perform a piece of your “art work”, depending on whether you are in the visual or performing arts sector. You will also be required to have a certain level of academic results. While creativity cannot be taught, your skills and talents can be fine-tuned with the help of a tertiary qualification. If you are in need of tertiary funding, these bursary opportunities could be very helpful. If the stage is your office, then these are the bursary listings for you. SA Bursaries has compiled the biggest list of Arts bursaries, scholarship and funding opportunities in South Africa for 2026 studies – scroll down to find your field of study: Dance is a disciplined art form whereby a dancer moves their body in a rhythmic way within a space and to music. The purpose of Dance is to express an idea or emotion through art, and release energy or simply enjoying the movement. Dance benefits society on a grand scale as it is significant both commercially and culturally. Many dancing colleges, schools, and studies play a major role in creating employment opportunities for local dancers. Dancing is also important to society as it creates social and cultural improvements to the communities and its people, and it is performed in and sometimes, bases its subject matter on. Both social and cultural interaction is enhanced through dance and communities are provided with a feeling of happiness and togetherness. To become a professional Dancer, you would need to obtain a Degree in Performing Arts and/ or attend a dance school where you will be examined as a professional. You can also attend any of South Africa’s Dance associations and complete an Associate dance teacher’s degree in your preferred style of dance. Some career opportunities for a professional dancer are a Choreographer, Choreologist, Dance Master, Dance Teacher, Dance Historian, and Dancer. If you are looking for bursaries or funding for the sector, here are some options for you: Drama and Theatre, although synonymous with each other, are actually different. Drama refers to scripts and is a part of theatre that illustrates an event or situation by the means of acting or performance. Drama also refers to an event or episode that stirred up emotion or conflict- i.e. the emotions of a performer who is acting. Theatre refers to the actual entirety of the play/ production and not JUST the script. Whilst Drama may only require the actors, theatre requires the works of the script, stage, actors, sound effects, lighting, costumes, audience, and more. With that, theatre requires more people such as stagehands, sound engineers, directors, and ushers to ensure the success of the production. Drama and theatre are essential to society, as it not only creates many job opportunities and is a source of entertainment, but also promotes social discussion and potential societal change. Theatre challenges society to look at its own actions as well as help it find solutions. To get a career in theatre, it is advisable that you study a Bachelor of Performing Arts or a Bachelor in Theatre and Performance. With this qualification, potential career avenues you could venture into are Choreography, Directing, becoming a critic, Performing, becoming a designer, a Production Arts Manager, Theatre-Maker, Teacher, a Writer, and Theorist. If you are looking for bursaries or funding for the sector, here are some options for you: Fashion and Textile design refer to creating a sophisticated and polished finished product and appearance- this can refer to clothing or interior design. Textile design refers to using various processes and techniques to create digital, constructed, and mixed media textiles. Textile refers to creating designs and then printing them onto various fabrics which can later be used in fashion and design. Fashion and textile are essential to society as textiles help make spaces feel more homely, welcoming, and professional as well as inviting to guests. Fashion helps the economy grow and is the second-largest worldwide economic activity for trade; it also can be a means for artists and designers to tell a story through their designs. The fashion and textile industry is also responsible for employing over 57 million workers in developing countries of which 80% are women. Qualifications you

title: Accounting Bursaries South Africa 2026
description_short: If you have a passion for numbers and for keeping order, you may be best suited to a career in the Finance/ Accounting sector. In general, Accountants are responsible for the balancing of books, creat
description_full: If you have a passion for numbers and for keeping order, you may be best suited to a career in the Finance/ Accounting sector. In general, Accountants are responsible for the balancing of books, creation and finalising of financial statements and all-round management of an organisations finances. For those who are interested in the various pay scales within the Finance/ Accounting field, here is an estimate of what you can expect to earn (based on years experience and location of the job): R95 000 to R 530 000/ annum R99 000 to R860 000/annum R110 000 to R390 000/annum R115 000 to R472 000/annum R120 000 to R458 000/ annum R150 000 to R780 000/annum R156 000 to R500 000/annum R186 000 to R590 000/annum R216 000 to R795 000/annum R218 000 to R742 000/annum R426 000 to R1 486 000/annum R495 000 to R1 550 000/annum R575 000 to R1 608 000/annum As qualified Accountants are considered one of the “scarce skills” in South Africa, there are many large corporations seeking to help students gain qualifications into the finance field by way of a bursary programme. Whether you intend on studying towards an accounting degree/ diploma/ certificate, here are a list of big companies offering accounting degrees and scholarships for South African students in need of financial assistance to complete their studies. Auditing is an on-site verification process, such as the examination or inspection of a quality system to ensure that it meets compliance requirements. An Auditors main responsibility in a business is to ensure that all financial records are accurately kept by reviewing all transactions systematically. The business owners have the responsibility to ensure that their accounting books are maintained, which Auditors then annually look at. Auditors are there to aid business owners in locating problems within their company. Once auditing is complete, the business will receive an audit report, explaining whether records were inaccurate, accurate, and/or missing. There are 2 types of Auditors in business: internal and external Auditors. Internal Auditors are appointed by the business owner to conduct auditing, thus checking company goals and preventing financial flaws. Internal Auditors examine business management and operations, as well as update board members and shareholders on the company’s finances. External auditing is auditing performed by a third party (e.g. SARS, insurance company, etc.). External audits may take a look at the business’ complete financial records or just various aspects of its operations. All reports done by external Auditors are compiled using auditing standards. To become an Auditor, you will need to obtain a relevant qualification (it is advised to work via an institution that has been endorsed by the IIA as they may offer internship programs.) Internships are fantastic as they can introduce you to the practical world of auditing. Next, you will need to become a member of a professional body (IIA SA brands), by doing this, you have an advantage as companies will want to employ branded professionals. You would then need to go through the IIA SA training program and then write the CIA exam/ specialty exam (if you wish to enhance your facilitation skills.) Lastly, you would need to adhere to the IIA SA requirements in order to maintain the professional IIA SA designations. If this sounds like a career path you would be interested in following, here are a list of to apply for: An Accountant and a Chartered Accountant (CA) are very similar, and really only have a few differences. The best way to understand this is that a Chartered Accountant is an Accountant with more experience, thus they are given a title. A Chartered Accountant has the responsibility to give financial advice, engage in auditing, and ensuring that financial records are secure and maintained. Some duties of a Charted Accountant are taxation, auditing, financial reporting, corporate finance, forensic accounting, insolvency, business recovery, and/or accounting processes and systems. Charted Accountant’ work in a number of businesses, with the common goal to enhance profitability for their employer/ client. To become a CA, you need to obtain excellent marks in Mathematics and Accounting in Matric. These will aid you in getting into a University where you would need to enroll for a Degree that is SAICA recognized to qualify to write a CTA. Once your Degree is completed and if your marks are high enough, you can then qualify to complete CTA. Next, you will need to sign up with a Training office and start your Learnership, and thereafter, you will need to complete 2 board exams. The first exam is the ITC (which you can write in 1 year after you pass your CTA) and then the APC. You will then become eligible after completing 18 months of your training contract. Upon successful completion of all of this, you will qualify as a Chartered Accountant! If this sounds like a career path you would be interested in following, here are a list of to

title: Vac Work – get working experience while you study
description_short: Vacation work is a great way for University students to gain valuable work experience whilst making a form of income during their university holidays. Most employers would prefer that you obtain some
description_full: Vacation work is a great way for University students to gain valuable work experience whilst making a form of income during their university holidays. Most employers would prefer that you obtain some working experience whilst you study. No matter the type of work (whether paid or voluntary) each can be seen as an opportunity to develop your skills and experience – something you can’t gain from just studies. Working in a new environment will also allow you the opportunity to create a solid network and gain useful contacts for your future working career. A perfect example of beneficial networking is the option to use your supervisor as a reference for future job applications after graduation. Vac work also allows you to develop interpersonal skills which will aid you in the workplace, such as communication, proactiveness, and attentiveness to workplace procedures. Whilst there are a number of benefits to engaging in vac work, here are researchers top 5 reasons you should get a job while you study: Through this, one can see how Vac work is worthwhile and beneficial to career advancement. It will not only allow you to get an extra source of income, but also build a sense of pride in yourself for putting in the effort to become a better version of yourself. We have compiled a list of companies that offer vac work – go through the list until you find a company that is the right match for you. Read our top tips on for the best chance of success. title: How to get Free Education in South Africa
company: How to get Free Education in
description_short: Tertiary education is very costly and unaffordable for most South Africans, but is highly necessary if you plan on entering a professional career such as Engineering, Accounting, Law, Medicine, Teachi
description_full: Tertiary education is very costly and unaffordable for most South Africans, but is highly necessary if you plan on entering a professional career such as Engineering, Accounting, Law, Medicine, Teaching, Science and more. Of course there is the option to take out a student loan with one of the big 4 banks, but once students have completed their studies and have found employment, this money must be paid back (with interest). The other obstacle to taking out a student loan is that you need a parent or guardian to stand surety for you. There is however another option for students who are in financial need but wish to pursue their tertiary studies, two means to free education: Bursaries are monetary awards granted by private organisations, government departments and study institutions. They are granted based on either academic performance or financial need (and sometimes both). These awards generally require students to “work back” the bursary award by taking up employment at the funding company once they have graduated. Coverage will differ between each bursary programme, but typically includes tuition fees, registration fees, examination fees, prescribed books and study materials, accommodation, meals, travel allowance and sometimes even a laptop computer. Apart from the financial aspect of the bursary award, many bursary providers also provide students with mentors, vacation work and practical on-the-job training. SA Bursaries has made it easy for students to access bursary opportunities and submit online bursary applications. NSFAS is a student bursary and loan scheme implemented and managed by the South African government and funded by the Department of Higher Education and Training. The funding is available to University and TVET students, and covers the cost of accommodation, transport, book allowances, living allowances and incidental/ personal care allowance. The value of the allowances differs based on certain criteria. Students who wish to apply for NSFAS funding must have South African citizenship, must be able to prove financial need by way of the NSFAS means test (students who are recipients automatically qualify for NSFAS funding), must have applied or intend on applying for studies at a TVET College or public University and must have a good academic record. ( ). Many universities in South Africa offer private scholarships and bursaries in specific faculties. The criteria of these awards differs by institution and students are encouraged to visit the universities website or contact the universities financial aid department for further information. Feenix is a South African crowdfunding platform, supported by Standard Bank, that was created to help students pay for their education. The platform offers students a simple and secure way to raise funds for their outstanding university fees. In order to register on the Feenix platform, students must be a South African citizen/ permanent resident/ refugee or asylum seeker. The student must be registered at a public South African university and must have current or historical debt owing to their university. Furthermore, students must have a total combined household income of less than R600 000 per year. Another similar crowdfunding platform is backabuddy. Lastly, if you are unable to secure funding via any of the above ways, a student loan may be an option for you. Student loans are generally provided by banks in South Africa and require that the student has a parent or guardian that receives an income, who can stand surety for them and service the interest of the loan. title: Tips to submitting a bursary application!
description_short: Applying for a bursary may seem daunting – so many documents to compile, a lengthy application to complete, and to top it off, a looming deadline. But not to fear, SA Bursaries, South Africa’s largest
description_full: Applying for a bursary may seem daunting – so many documents to compile, a lengthy application to complete, and to top it off, a looming deadline. But not to fear, SA Bursaries, South Africa’s largest bursary website is here to help. We offer you the following tips to help you put your best foot forwards when submitting your bursary application: First off, make sure that you have all of the supporting documentation that is required for the bursary you are applying for – generally this includes your ID document; latest academic record; proof of tertiary application and acceptance, but sometimes you will also be required to submit additional documentation such as your parents or guardians proof of income and proof of residence. Once you’ve compiled all your documents, make sure that they are certified – this can be done be anyone who is a commissioner of oaths at your local police station, post office or bank. Also remember that you should only be submitting copies of your documents (and not the originals), as documents will not be returned to applicants. This should be quite obvious, but if you are submitting your application via post, you need to ensure that you submit in time that your application reaches the bursary office before the deadline. Under no circumstances are late application accepted. (See which bursaries are currently open for application here: ) Honesty is vital when applying for a bursary – reference, credit and criminal checks will be conducted by the bursary provider before any bursary agreements are signed. Also, don’t forget to double and triple check that you have not left any fields blank on the application form – applicants will not be contacted to confirm any missing information, so omitting information could be detrimental to your application. What sets you apart from other bursary applicants? Why do you deserve to be awarded the bursary more than the next candidate? How will getting a bursary change your future? How will you contribute to society when you have completed your studies? These are all things you should think about when writing a motivational letter to support your application. (read our tips on how to ). References are a pillar to strength for your application, so make sure that you have references with good standing – this can include a school principle, community leader or other authority figure. Verify with your reference whether they would be comfortable with supporting your application and make sure that you submit their correct contact details (telephone number and email address). Students that have been shortlisted for the bursary, will receive feedback in the form of an email, SMS or telephone call, so make sure you don’t miss that important communication. Shortlisted candidates are generally required to attend an interview before final selection is made, so make sure that you show up on time. Don’t forget to check which and read our (including how to , how to , our and more).

title: National Benchmark Test (NBT) for University Entrance
company: National Benchmark Test (NBT) for University Entrance
description_short: – essentially it tests their readiness for university study. The test is used to measure the students ability to transfer understanding of language skills, numerical skills and mathematics skills to m
description_full: – essentially it tests their readiness for university study. The test is used to measure the students ability to transfer understanding of language skills, numerical skills and mathematics skills to meet the demands of higher education coursework. The NBT has 2 types of tests, which each measure different skills, as stated above. Each test requires candidates to use their prior knowledge and experience, to assess whether first year students meet the expectations of the specific university programmes. The NBTP was commissioned in 2005 by the HESA (Higher Education Statistics Agency). Consists of 2 sections that are reported individually. The test determines: The test determines: The AQL test is generally written for all programmes, while the MAT test is generally written for programmes that require Mathematics (Engineering, Accounting, Health Science and other Sciences). This will be determined by each University and faculty. Every University has its own admission criteria and policies – students should check with their specific institution whether the NBT tests are required (this can be found on university websites and in their prospectus). NBT tests are held online and in-person. Before writing the NBT test, candidates must follow these instructions: First, you will need to ( ). Candidates must have the following when registering: The username (ID or Passport number) and password are used to: complete the registration process if you did not finish, correct personal information and request change to test date or site. The EasyPay number during registration is used to verify payment so that your results may be released. Candidates will receive confirmation of their test venue 2 days before the test (via SMS and email). Learners who are unable to write at the physical site (due to being in another country or in an area too remote to travel to the site), may complete the test online, under the guidance of a test proctor/ invigilator. Writers taking the test online, must do so in a secure proctored online environment. Learners will be monitored throughout the test to ensure they follow all testing procedures. The test session will be recorded. Find out how to Important: read the following documents if you will be doing the online test: If you are registering to write the NBT’s online, you will be required to complete the compulsory Simulation (after registering). The simulation must be completed by the Wednesday before your online test date: https://cetap.edtest.ai/ Watch the recording of the (skip ahead to 07:00 minutes) The 2023 prices are: On test day, candidates must have the following items with them: Please do NOT bring the following items: The NBT is available in English and Afrikaans (the common languages of instruction used in South African Institutions). Afrikaans tests are only available on specific dates at test sites across South Africa. Students with disabilities may write the test in Braille. Candidates can prepare by reviewing the NBT exemplar questions (these are just an indication as to the type of questions that will be asked in the test). Institutions will receive results within 4 weeks of candidates writing the test. Candidates will be able to access their results on the NBT website after 5 weeks. The NBT help desk is available to answer any questions, from Monday to Friday (08:00 to 16:00) and on test weekends (07:00 to 10:00). Contact them on 021 650 3523 Please read the before making any queries. Read our top tips on for the best chance of success. Read our tips on here. Read about how to here. Read about the .

description_short: Admission Point Score (APS) is used to determine whether students can gain entrance into university or college – it is calculated using your Matric marks. The marks are separated into percentage brack
description_full: Admission Point Score (APS) is used to determine whether students can gain entrance into university or college – it is calculated using your Matric marks. The marks are separated into percentage brackets, and each bracket has a number of points. Once you know how many points you have for each subject, you can add them together to get your APS. Here is what you need to know about APS calculations: Matric Subject Symbol / Mark Obtained in Matric exam = APS (Admission Point Score) Here is an example of calculated APS score according to Matric final results: Tsepho received these results for his Matric NSC final exams and wants to know what his APS score is: His total APS is 32. With this APS result, Tsepho will gain a Matric exemption and be able to pursue Bachelor degree studies at university. There are 6 subjects that will count towards your APS (you will complete a minimum of 7 subjects in Matric, but remember that Life Orientation is excluded in the APS score). Your APS score will be made up of The APS you want to get will depend on what you want to study and where you want to study. The minimum APS requirements for the four passing levels are as follows: The highest APS you can get is 42. This is because Life Orientation does not count, and so you will be calculating your APS using six subjects. The highest number of points you can get for one subject is 7. A Bachelor’s Degree Pass is the highest Matric pass you can achieve. Many students who earn distinctions usually achieve a Bachelors pass. The minimum requirements to get a Bachelor’s Degree Pass is as follows: The minimum requirements to get this certificate is as follows: A Higher Certificate Pass allows students to enrol at a college or distance learning institution. The minimum requirements to get this certificate is as follows: The minimum requirements to get this certificate are as follows: While having a good APS score is important, simply meeting the pass mark doesn’t guarantee admission into a university. Universities will look at other important criteria and basic requirements that need to be met by students. This includes subjects taken in school and attaining a bachelor’s pass. It should be noted that Life Orientation is excluded from the APS calculation. Universities will also have to evaluate the amount of space available for the course. Furthermore, some students might even have to write an entrance exam, depending on the course. A student’s chance of getting accepted improves once they have passed the National benchmarking test. All of the above factors play a detrimental role in whether or not a student will get accepted. At the end of the day, your APS score can help you determine which career path to take. title: Preparing for a Bursary Interview
description_short: Bursaries are a great opportunity for students to fund their learning. Although they are competitive and applications don’t guarantee an interview, there are still a number of steps applicants can tak
description_full: Bursaries are a great opportunity for students to fund their learning. Although they are competitive and applications don’t guarantee an interview, there are still a number of steps applicants can take to ensure that they plan ahead and prepare well for their potential interview. Firstly, applicants should always they are applying for. They should understand the requirements needed for the application and keep a copy of everything they submit for reference. Application questions for bursary programmes give candidates a great understanding of what they potentially could be asked on the day as well as allow them to review any answers they may need to give the provider in the interview. The next step applicants should do is . The applicant can do this by visiting the providers’ website and reading their ‘About Us’ section. This will allow the applicant to gain a key understanding of the providers’ priorities, goals, mission, vision, as well as what they are looking for in a successful candidate. Below are some of the . The applicant can use these questions to prepare for their interview so that their answers are ones that best describe them and show their strongest attributes. The applicant should consider factors such as what the adequate response will be to answer a question in depth. Applicants should ensure that their answers are not vague, and should give responses that are solid and confident. Although preparation is a necessity, the applicant should also ensure that their answers are not too robotic or rehearsed. An interview is not only an opportunity to showcase your skills and professionalism, but also show your spontaneity and natural responses to questions. The applicant can achieve the ‘sweet spot’ between rehearsing and spontaneousness by practising with a friend. The applicant should also prepare and get an understanding of questions they may want to ask the provider – this will allow the applicant to get a better understanding of the programme, as well as gain some additional information. Read our other here. View our here.

title: How to write a Bursary Motivational Letter
description_short: A bursary is a financial award, offered to students who are unable to afford their full tuition fees or those who have excelled academically. Bursaries are awarded based on various criteria, including
description_full: A bursary is a financial award, offered to students who are unable to afford their full tuition fees or those who have excelled academically. Bursaries are awarded based on various criteria, including academic results, financial need and other personal attributes (leadership skills, community involvement etc). Because companies receive many applications for their bursary programme, it is vital that applicants write an honest and compelling motivational letter, to give the best first impression and set them apart from other applicants. The motivational letter should explain why you are in need of financial and how the opportunity will help you both financially and professionally. A motivational application letter is very similar to a Curriculum Vitae cover letter as it states your goals, accomplishments as well as a brief description of your credentials- this is essential as it can put you ahead of your competition. It is vital that you genuinely and honestly show your need for financial aid in your bursary letter, explaining how the award would help fund your education. The companies offering the programmes use these letters to decide on a suitable recipient and base their choice on need, merit, or both, depending on the specific requirements the bursary has. At the top left corner of the letter, include the address of the bursary provider (you can also include the date you are applying on, your name, and contact details). The should purposefully state the reason for your application. It should be no longer than 2 or 3 sentences and should include a clear thesis similar to the following: “I am applying for the [insert company name] bursary programme to help fund my [course of study] qualification, for the [course year study] academic year. The should elaborate on your education and include your personal achievements, current studies, and plans you have for further education (example, a graduate degree.) Be precise and to the point in this paragraph and ensure that it too, is no longer than 3 sentences. The should include your career plans and explain how your education and degree complement them. This section should reflect on your personal goals as well as explain how the bursary will aid you in covering certain funds. You could briefly include the circumstances that lead to you needing financial need, but do not linger on it. This paragraph should focus mainly on your plans for the future. With all this in mind, this is a paragraph that can be a little longer than the rest. The of your motivational letter should be short, stating your gratitude for the consideration and opportunity. Finally, , “Sincerely, (your name).” Read our top tips on for the best chance of success. Read our tips on here. Read about how to here. Read about the . Read about the .

raw_data.title: Scholarships and Bursaries for Primary School Students
raw_data.company: and Bursaries for Primary School Students
raw_data.description_short: Scholarships and bursaries are a great way to kickstart your child’s secondary/ tertiary school career at a private institution. Many prestigious schools aim to attract only the best students, with mo
raw_data.description_full: Scholarships and bursaries are a great way to kickstart your child’s secondary/ tertiary school career at a private institution. Many prestigious schools aim to attract only the best students, with most offering some form of financial assistance to bright students whose parents can not afford the tuition. If you are looking for high school bursaries, visit our page. A scholarship is a financial award given to talented and hardworking students. Scholarships are most often funded by donations or legacies from previous pupils and other benefactors. Owing to this, older and established private schools will often have more scholarships to offer than that of public, or newer institutions. This is because a scholarship award is based on the academic excellence/ talent of the student. There are some schools that offer scholarships that cover 100% of fees, but this is not common. Generally, most scholarships are worth 20 to 40% of the students yearly fees. Bursaries are different from scholarships, as Bursaries are aimed at academically gifted and talented students who may not have the financial means to study at a private school. Bursaries are often awarded based on the “means-test” and are more valuable than scholarships, as they cover full or partial fees with any surplus of funds covering extras (like uniforms and school outings). Students may be able to qualify for both forms of funding (scholarships and bursaries.) In this instance, the child will be placed at the chosen private institution and receive financial aid (owing to their ability.) The bursary, however, will enable them to attend their chosen institution. Academic scholarships are awarded every year to the best performing students. Often, , with the scholarship being awarded to the students who have obtained top marks. Thereafter, students are not often required to take a separate exam. However, should a student be applying for a scholarship based on their aptitude in a specific subject, it is expected that they will have to prove their talents. Examples are taking an additional test for a specific subject (such as Mathematics), auditioning for acting/ music, demonstrating their sporting ability, etc. You might also be required to give details of your assets as well as list other family members that are dependent on the income, and give a description of your monthly expenses. This means that while bursaries are awarded based on a means-test, it is not always straightforward (e.g. a family that has a higher income but more expenses may qualify more a family with a lower income.) Some schools do, however, automatically grant bursaries to the students most in need, whereas some require the students to apply for them on their own accord. It’s important to note that many schools award bursary awards not solely on financial need, but also based on the ability and talents of the student. Therefore, if your child is in financial need but their exam marks are not the best, then they may not qualify for any award. SA Bursaries has compiled a list of funding offered for primary school learners (please note that this list is being added to regularly, so please check back here soon): offers 3 types of bursaries: 1. The General Bursary Fund for previously disadvantaged individuals, to attend St Peter’s Schools from Grade 3 to Grade 7 2. The Old Girls’ Legacy Bursary Fund for daughters or granddaughters of old girls who would like to attend St Peter’s in future years, but may be unable to pay the school fees 3. The Helen Atkinson Bursary Fund, to educate the children of St Peter’s support staff at a school outside of St Peter’s , offers a scholarship fund to at least 1 learner per year. The placement goes to a deserving boy from a disadvantaged background, whose family would otherwise be unable to afford a SACS education. are located in all provinces across South Africa, as well as in Botswana and Namibia. Curro offers a few different school scholarship to help learners gain a high-quality education. offers a school fee rebate to parents who cannot afford the fees. The bursary is granted based on the academic performance of the learner and the financial situation of the family. offer funding to students through the George Bizos SAHETI Scholarship and Bursary Fund (GBSSBF). The Scholarship and Bursary was established on the initiative of Advocate George Bizos and his initial financial contribution, to assist and attract learners of outstanding ability who are in financial need. , offers a limited number of bursaries for Grades 5, 6 and 7. The bursary award is for a partial tuition fee remission. offers the CHELO Bursary Support Programme. CHELO is a registered NPO created in 2007 by a group of parents partnered with HBIS. School fees are contributed by HBIS, while CHELO funds all other direct (financial, social & educational) costs of the bursary. , offers numerous bursaries, based on financial need and talent, and where possible, in partnership with outside sponsor

raw_data.title: High School Bursaries and Scholarships
raw_data.description_short: Access to quality education in South Africa often comes with significant financial pressure, especially at the high school level where fees, uniforms, books, and extracurricular costs can quickly add
raw_data.description_full: Access to quality education in South Africa often comes with significant financial pressure, especially at the high school level where fees, uniforms, books, and extracurricular costs can quickly add up. For many families, , as they help talented and hardworking learners attend schools that might otherwise be out of reach. These funding options are not only designed to support academic excellence, but also to recognise leadership, sporting ability, cultural achievements, and financial need. Whether you’re a parent planning ahead or a student aiming to secure your future, understanding how high school bursaries and scholarships work is an important first step. From prestigious private school awards to government and organisation-funded support, South Africa offers a wide range of options. In this guide, we’ll explore the different types available, who qualifies, and how to apply, so you can make informed decisions and maximise your chances of success. We have compiled a list of bursaries and scholarships available for high school students: If you are looking for primary school bursaries, visit our page. Don’t forget to check which and read our (including how to , how to , our and more).

raw_data.description_full: KwaZulu-Natal Department of Social Development invites unemployed residents to apply for Expanded Public Works Programme (EPWP) Participant Opportunities 2026 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); EPWP Application Closing Date: 20 May 2026 EPWP Location: KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa EPWP Opportunity Details Organisation: KwaZulu-Natal Department of Social Development Programme: Expanded Public Works Programme (EPWP) Participants Number of Posts: 519 Contract Duration: 10 Months Monthly Stipend: R2 786.08 Minimum Qualification: ABET Application Method: Hand-delivered applications only Province: KwaZulu-Natal (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The KwaZulu-Natal Department of Social Development is offering EPWP Participant posts across various districts and service offices in the province. This opportunity is aimed at unemployed residents who want to gain work experience while supporting public service delivery. What Is the EPWP Programme? The Expanded Public Works Programme is a government initiative that creates temporary work opportunities for unemployed people. The programme helps participants gain workplace experience, improve employability, earn a monthly stipend and contribute to community development. Although EPWP posts are temporary, they can help applicants build references, learn workplace discipline and gain practical experience for future job opportunities. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Duties and Responsibilities Successful applicants will mainly perform cleaning and general support duties. Duties include: Providing cleaning services, cleaning general kitchen areas, cleaning restrooms, and keeping and maintaining cleaning materials and equipment. Monthly Stipend Successful EPWP Participants will receive: R2 786.08 per month Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); ABET qualification, basic knowledge of operating cleaning equipment, verbal communication skills, interpersonal skills, reliability and a hard-working attitude. This opportunity is suitable for unemployed people with limited formal qualifications who want to gain work experience in the public sector. Important Application Rules Applicants must note the following: Applications must be hand-delivered, applicants must use the new Z83 form, the Z83 form must be fully completed and signed, and applications must include a detailed Curriculum Vitae. Certified copies of qualifications are not required during the application stage. Only shortlisted candidates will be requested to submit certified documents. Failure to follow the application instructions may result in disqualification. Security and Verification Checks Successful candidates may be subjected to security clearance, citizenship verification, qualification verification, criminal record checks, credit record checks and previous employment verification.  Midvaal Local Municipality invites unemployed residents to apply for  EPWP General Worker
raw_data.description_full: Midvaal Local Municipality invites unemployed residents to apply for EPWP General Worker: Public Safety & Roads and Engineering Services x30 Opportunities 2026 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); EPWP Application Closing Date: 21 May 2026 at 12:00 EPWP Location: Meyerton, Midvaal Local Municipality, Gauteng, South Africa EPWP Opportunity Details Position: EPWP General Worker: Public Safety & Roads and Engineering Services Number of Posts: 30 Company: Midvaal Local Municipality Contract Duration: Fixed Term Contract of 2 years Daily Rate: R156.88 per day Location: Meyerton, Gauteng (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Midvaal Local Municipality is participating in the Expanded Public Works Programme (EPWP) to help reduce unemployment and poverty by creating work opportunities for unemployed people. Available Sections Applicants must clearly indicate which section they are interested in when submitting their application. The available EPWP General Worker positions are: Roads and Storm Water Section: 20 positions Engineering Services Department: 10 positions (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The Engineering Services posts are within the Water, Purification and Sewer Sections . Requirements Applicants must have: Grade 10, ability to understand English, and must be bilingual. Applicants must reside within the jurisdiction of Midvaal Local Municipality. Applicants must also be able to follow and execute instructions, be in excellent physical health, have basic skills and understanding of the work area, and be able to perform physically demanding work in different weather conditions. Applicants must meet the operational requirements of the post, be hardworking and self-disciplined. Applicants must have at least 0 to 1 year experience in general worker, labourer or labour-intensive duties. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Applicants must be unemployed or serving the last three months of a contracted term. Duties and Responsibilities Successful applicants will be responsible for labour-intensive duties, including loading and off-loading materials, tools and equipment used for daily tasks. Other duties include placing traffic signs to warn motorists about work being done next to municipal roads, cleaning in and around the working area, and using various manual labour equipment and tools. Applicants may also be required to operate manual and electrical tools to perform labour-related work. Successful candidates must execute lawful instructions from supervisors to ensure productivity and the smooth running of the section. They may also be required to perform other related ad hoc tasks and duties as requested by management or supervisors. Important Notes for Applicants Due to operational requirements, the EPWP roles advertised are available for male candidates only . The roles are elementary and aimed at attracting unskilled and semi-skilled applicants. The opportunity is not ward-specific, and all residents of Midvaal are encouraged to apply. Specific preference will be given to youth between the ages of 18 and 35 . Applicants who reside in neighbouring municipalities such as Lesedi Local Municipality, Emfuleni Local Municipality, Ekurhuleni Metropolitan Municipality, City of Johannesburg, City of Tshwane, Mogale City, West Rand District and Sedibeng District will be disqualified. Any applicant who provides false or misrepresented information will be disqualified. Incomplete, unclear or illegible CVs will not be considered. Applications without CVs will also be disqualified. Employment Equity Preference will be given to applicants who promote representativeness in terms of Employment Equity targets. Midvaal Local Municipality is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. Background Checks Shortlisted candidates will be subjected to background screening checks, including qualification verification, criminal record checks and security vetting. Candidates with foreign qualifications must submit a letter from SAQA stating the equivalence of the qualification. How to Apply Click here to apply for Midvaal Local Municipality EPWP General Worker Opportunities 2026 . Human Resources Enquiries: Tel: 016 360 7435 / 7432 / 7569 / 7557 Due to the large number of applications expected, applications will not be acknowledged. If you do not receive any response within three months, please accept that your application was unsuccessful. NB: The Council reserves the right not to make an appointment to the above positions.

raw_data.description_full: Polokwane Municipality invites suitably qualified unemployed graduates to apply for Cashier Vacancies 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Application Closing Date: 22 May 2026 at 12:30 Job Location: Polokwane, Limpopo, South Africa Polokwane Municipality Cashier Vacancies 2026 Polokwane Municipality is recruiting 15 Cashiers within the Directorate: Transportation Services on a 12-month fixed-term contract. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Successful candidates will assist with fare revenue administration, customer service, and travel card sales within municipal transport operations. Position Details Cashier x15 Posts Directorate: Transportation Services Salary: R10 000 per month Contract Duration: 12 Months Fixed-Term Contract Reference Number: EXT/25/26/130 Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: National Diploma or Degree in: Accounting Financial Management Internal Auditing Added Advantage 0–2 years’ experience in: Leeto la Polokwane Bus Operations Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) systems Competencies and Skills Candidates should demonstrate: Cash handling and numeracy skills Customer service orientation Good communication skills Honesty and integrity Reliability and professionalism Duties and Responsibilities Successful candidates will be responsible for: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Selling LLP Travel Cards at designated sales points Fare revenue administration Assisting customers with enquiries Providing quality customer service Capturing transactions accurately on the system Working closely with supervisors and staff members to ensure smooth operations Required Documents Applicants must upload: Detailed CV Certified copies of qualifications Certified copy of ID Proof of residence Supporting documents Certified copies must not be older than six months. How to Apply Apply Online Faxed and emailed applications will not be considered. Enquiries Human Resources Management Contact Person: Ms. Ouma Napo Telephone: (015) 290 2116 / 2004 / 2031 Important Notes Preference will be given to applicants residing within the City of Polokwane jurisdiction area. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. If you do not receive feedback within four months after the closing date, consider your application unsuccessful. Polokwane Municipality reserves the right to cancel the advertisement at any stage. The municipality does not charge any application fees.

 University is not only about academics
raw_data.description_full: University is not only about academics. Many South African institutions such as the University of Pretoria, University of Johannesburg, and Stellenbosch University offer excellent sports opportunities for students. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Whether you are a professional athlete or simply enjoy staying active, university sports can help you build skills, friendships, and even career opportunities. Here are 10 important facts about university sports opportunities every student should know. 1. Universities Offer Many Different Sports Most universities offer sports such as: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Soccer Rugby Netball Athletics Basketball Cricket Hockey Volleyball Some institutions also provide niche sports and recreational activities. 2. Sports Scholarships Are Available Talented athletes may qualify for: Sports bursaries Scholarships Financial support These opportunities can help cover tuition, accommodation, and sports expenses. 3. You Can Compete Nationally University teams often compete in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Varsity Sports tournaments Inter-university leagues National championships These competitions attract scouts and sponsors. 4. Sports Can Improve Your CV Participating in sports helps students develop: Leadership skills Teamwork Discipline Time management These qualities are valued by employers. 5. Universities Have Professional Facilities Many universities have: Modern gyms Sports fields Swimming pools Fitness centres High-performance training facilities 6. You Don’t Need to Be a Professional Athlete University sports are open to: Competitive athletes Recreational players Beginners Many campuses have social leagues and fitness clubs. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); 7. Sports Help Reduce Stress Exercise and sports participation can improve: Mental health Focus Energy levels This helps students balance academic pressure. 8. Some Universities Are Known for Sports Excellence Institutions like: University of Pretoria Stellenbosch University North-West University are recognised for strong sports programmes. 9. Sports Can Create Career Opportunities University sports can open doors to: Professional sports careers Coaching opportunities Sports management careers Networking opportunities 10. Balancing Sports and Academics is Important Student-athletes must learn to balance: Training Competitions Classes Assignments Time management is essential for success. Final Thoughts University sports offer much more than physical activity. They provide opportunities for growth, scholarships, networking, and personal development. Whether you want to compete professionally or simply stay active, participating in university sports can make your student experience more exciting and rewarding.

raw_data.description_full: Department of Land Reform and Rural Development invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the National Rural Youth Service Corps (NARYSEC) Trainee Programme 2026 . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Programme Application Closing Date: 25 May 2026 Programme Location: Johannes Phumani Phungula Local Municipality, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa About the NARYSEC Programme The National Rural Youth Service Corps (NARYSEC) Programme was established to empower young people in rural areas by providing skills development, training, and community service opportunities. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The programme is aligned with the National Development Plan (Vision 2030) and focuses on: Developing skilled rural youth Promoting economic participation Supporting youth-led development initiatives Successful candidates will attend a 9-week induction and Youth Leadership Development Programme , followed by practical training and community service. Available Training Opportunities Applicants can apply for training in the following fields: Firefighter Trainee Traffic Officer Trainee Minimum Requirements Applicants must: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Be between 18 and 35 years old Have a Grade 12 certificate or NQF Level 4 Reside within Johannes Phumani Phungula Local Municipality (all wards) Be unemployed and not studying Have no criminal record Be medically fit (physical assessment required) Have a valid driver’s licence (Code B for Traffic Officer) Have a pass in English (40%) Have a pass in Mathematics Literacy (40%) Be willing to undergo training away from home Note: Post-matric qualifications may apply, but preference will be given to applicants whose highest qualification is Grade 12. Required Documents Applicants must submit: Certified copy of South African ID Proof of residence (from Traditional Council or Municipal Councillor) Grade 12 certificate or statement of results Updated CV with at least 2 contactable references Completed and signed application form How to Apply Click here to apply for NARYSEC Trainee Programme 2026: Application forms are available at Johannes Phumani Phungula Local Municipality, 29 Margaret Street, Ixopo, 3276 (Reception) Submission Method: Submit completed application forms physically in the application box at the same address Ensure all supporting documents are attached Enquiries: Contact: Mrs Bulelwa Nzimande Phone: 082 969 6765 Email: Bulelwa.Nzimande@dlrrd.gov.za Important Notes Applications must be submitted physically (no online/email submissions) Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted If you do not receive feedback within 3 months after closing, consider your application unsuccessful Successful applicants will undergo medical and security screening This programme is a great opportunity for young people in rural KwaZulu-Natal to gain valuable skills in firefighting or traffic services , while contributing to community development and improving their future employment prospects. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

raw_data.description_full: PepsiCo invites South African students to apply for the R&D Work Integrated Learning (WIL) Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Location: Isando, South Africa Reference Number: 444068 Field: Research & Development (R&D) About the PepsiCo R&D WIL Learnership The PepsiCo R&D WIL Learnership (Seasoning) is designed for students in their final year who need practical workplace experience to complete their qualification. The programme focuses on research and development within food production, particularly seasoning applications, pilot plant trials, and product testing. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learners will gain hands-on experience in real production environments while supporting R&D Technologists on various projects. Key Responsibilities As part of the programme, successful candidates will: Assist with trial protocols and coordinate with stakeholders Support research and development projects within the SA R&D team (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Help execute pilot plant and factory production trials Conduct laboratory tests such as: Salt analysis Oil analysis Bulk density analysis Particle size analysis Moisture analysis Support product development and seasoning applications Assist in compiling project timelines and reports Work with regulatory teams to ensure compliance Participate in health, safety, and hygiene (GMP) practices (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Minimum Requirements To qualify for this learnership, applicants must: Be a South African citizen Be in their final year or have completed the theoretical part of their qualification Have a minimum average of 60% Be studying in one of the following fields: Biochemistry Food Technology Engineering Have strong analytical and technical skills Be a team player with good communication skills Be self-motivated, organized, and eager to learn Advantageous Skills Having the following will be beneficial: Valid driver’s license Basic knowledge of statistics Ability to work independently and handle complex tasks Strong collaboration and problem-solving skills Creative thinking and innovation ability How to Apply Click here to apply for PepsiCo R&D WIL Learnership 2026: https://www.pepsicojobs.com/main/jobs/444068?lang=en-us&iisn=linkedin Why You Should Apply This learnership offers valuable industry exposure in food science and product development. You will gain practical experience in a global company, improving your chances of securing permanent employment in the R&D or food manufacturing sector. Meta Description PepsiCo R&D WIL Learnership 2026 in Isando now open for South African students in Biochemistry, Food Technology, or Engineering. Apply now and gain practical experience.

raw_data.description_full: Lancet Laboratories invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Pre-Analytical Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 12 May 2026 Internship Location: South Africa About the Lancet Internship Programme Lancet Laboratories is offering a Pre-Analytical Internship opportunity for individuals interested in gaining experience within the pathology and healthcare environment. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The internship focuses on supporting laboratory operations through sample sorting, registration, distribution, and tracking while ensuring compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs). This programme provides practical exposure to laboratory support services and healthcare administration processes. Internship Overview Interns will assist the Pre-Analytical team with: Sorting and distributing pathology samples Registering and tracking specimens Supporting laboratory administration Maintaining workplace cleanliness and organisation Assisting with courier and specimen dispatch processes Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Grade 12 or relevant NQF Level 4 qualification Advantageous: BSc Degree Experience Required 0–1 year relevant working experience Key Responsibilities Sample Reception and Laboratory Support Sort and distribute pathology samples according to SOPs Assist with sample registration and logging Verify sample information on the Laboratory Information System (LIS) Scan patient forms onto the Occulus laboratory scanning system Assist with specimen dispatch and monitor courier progress Customer Service Interact professionally with medical staff and stakeholders Maintain ethical and professional customer care standards Workplace Standards Perform housekeeping and cleaning duties Prepare and maintain IT equipment according to SOPs Required Skills and Competencies Attention to detail Communication skills Administrative abilities Ethical behaviour and confidentiality Ability to work under pressure Teamwork skills Ability to work with distressed or nervous patients Additional Knowledge Requirements Computer literacy Laboratory Information Systems (LIS) knowledge Understanding of organisational policies and procedures How to Apply Click here to apply for Lancet Laboratories Pre-Analytical Internship 2026: https://careers.lancet.co.za/vacancy-details.aspx?vacancyRef=1167084 This internship is an excellent opportunity for individuals seeking experience in the healthcare and pathology sector , while developing practical administrative and laboratory support skills in a professional medical environment.

raw_data.description_full: Southern Sphere Platinum invites South African students to apply for the Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 6 April 2026 (at 17:00) Bursary Location: Sekhukhune District, Limpopo, South Africa About Southern Sphere Platinum Southern Sphere Platinum is a mining exploration and development company focusing on platinum group metals (PGMs) and chrome in the Steelpoort region. The company is committed to uplifting local communities by investing in education and skills development, especially for students pursuing careers in the mining and related industries. Fields Covered The bursary is available for students studying in the following fields: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Chemical Engineering Data Science Electrical Engineering Environmental Science / Environmental Management / Ecology / Conservation Biology Geology Information Technology / Computer Science Legal Mechanical Engineering Metallurgical Engineering Mining Engineering Process Engineering Bursary Benefits The bursary covers expenses up to R200 000 per student , including: Tuition fees Prescribed textbooks Accommodation (residence or approved housing) Meal allowance Note: The bursary does not cover outstanding fees from previous years. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be a South African citizen Must reside in Sekhukhune District (Limpopo) Must be studying in one of the listed fields Must be entering 2nd year of study in 2026 Must have a minimum average of 60% in 1st year Must be studying at one of these universities: University of Limpopo North-West University University of the Witwatersrand University of Johannesburg University of Pretoria Must not be receiving another bursary Preference given to students with financial need Required Documents Submit the following documents: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Motivational letter Certified copy of ID Matric certificate Full academic record (1st year) Proof of registration/acceptance Proof of residence in Sekhukhune District Selection Process Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within 21 days after the closing date Additional assessments (psychometric tests or interviews) may be required How to Apply Send your application via email: 📧 bursaries@southernsphere.co.za 📌 Use subject line: “Southern Sphere Platinum Bursary 2026”

raw_data.description_full: South African Road Federation invites students to apply for the SARF Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Closing Date: 31 August 2026 Funding Type: Partial (Supplementary Bursary) Study Level: Diploma, Degree, and Postgraduate Institutions: South African universities and Universities of Technology About the SARF Bursary Programme The SARF Bursary Programme is a strategic funding opportunity aimed at developing skills in South Africa’s road transport and infrastructure sector . This bursary supports students pursuing careers in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Road engineering Transport management Transport economics Road safety Unlike many bursaries, SARF strongly encourages postgraduate studies (MSc, MTech and beyond) , making it ideal for students aiming to become specialists in infrastructure and transport systems. Fields Covered Road Engineering (Civil) Traffic engineering Transportation planning Pavement and bridge engineering Construction materials and management Environmental and stormwater engineering Road Transport Management Logistics coordination Fleet operations Transport systems optimisation Freight planning Transport Economics Infrastructure investment analysis Transport policy development Cost-benefit modelling Freight corridor planning Road Safety Engineering Accident prevention systems Traffic flow design Smart road technologies Highway safety assessments Who Should Apply? You should apply if you are: Studying civil engineering (transport focus) Studying logistics or transport management Studying transport economics Studying road safety engineering Planning postgraduate studies in infrastructure Minimum Requirements Diploma Students: Must have passed Semester 1 Degree Students: Must have passed first year Postgraduate Students: Preference given to MSc, MTech, and advanced research students Institution Requirements Applicants must: Study at a recognised South African institution Be endorsed by their academic institution Proof of enrolment Academic performance Programme suitability Funding Coverage The SARF bursary is a supplementary bursary , meaning: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Amount varies per student Determined by SARF Council Paid directly to your institution Covers part of study costs Students are encouraged to combine funding with: NSFAS University bursaries SETA funding Employer sponsorship Selection Preference Preference is given to applicants who: Demonstrate strong academic performance Plan careers in the transport sector Work for SARF member organisations Show commitment to infrastructure development South African residents are prioritised, but SADC applicants may also apply . Important Requirement Applicants must show commitment to working in the South African road transport sector after completing their studies. Required Documents Prepare the following: Certified ID copy Academic transcripts Proof of registration CV Motivation letter Institutional endorsement letter How to Apply Request and submit your application form via: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Address: South African Road Federation 48 Gladiator Street Rhodesfield Kempton Park, 1619 Postal Address: PO Box 8379 Birchleigh, 1621 Contact: Tel: 011 394 9025 / 011 394 1459 Email: sybul@sarf.org.za Important Notes Closing date: 31 August 2026 Late applications are not accepted Ensure all documents are complete before submission

raw_data.description_full: Sasol invites South African students to apply for the Mainstream Bursary Programme for the 2027 academic year. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Opening Date: 01 April 2026 Bursary Application Closing Date: 17 May 2026 Study Location: Recognised South African Universities Bursary Overview The Sasol Mainstream Bursary Programme 2027 offers a comprehensive funding package for students pursuing full-time university studies in engineering and science fields . The bursary includes: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Full financial support (tuition, accommodation, etc.) Developmental support and mentorship Possible employment through Sasol’s Graduate Development Programmes ⚠️ Note: This bursary includes a workback/payback obligation after completion of studies. Fields of Study Undergraduate (BEng / BSc) Applicants may study the following: Chemical Engineering Civil Engineering Electrical Engineering Electronic Engineering Industrial Engineering Mechanical Engineering Mining Engineering Metallurgical Engineering Chemistry (up to Honours level required) Geology (up to Honours level required) Data Science (Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Operations Research) Undergraduate (BEng Tech) Mine Surveying (University of Technology only) Postgraduate (MSc / MEng / PhD) Fields include: Chemical Engineering (Fluid Dynamics, Thermodynamics, Process Modelling, etc.) Chemistry (Catalysis, Polymer Science, Environmental Science, Analytical Chemistry) Cyber Security Data Science (Applied Mathematics, Statistics) Minimum Requirements Applicants must: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Be a South African citizen Be under 30 years of age Intend to study full-time at a recognised South African university Have strong academic results ⚠️ Important: Mathematics (not Maths Literacy) is required No distance learning (e.g., UNISA not accepted) Only degree programmes (no diplomas) Important Conditions Only one application per applicant is allowed Applications must be submitted online only Incorrect or incomplete information will result in disqualification Selection is highly competitive Bursaries are awarded based on academic performance and Sasol business needs Required Documents Applicants must ensure they submit: Academic results (Grade 11 or latest results) Certified ID copy Any additional required supporting documents How to Apply Click here to apply for Sasol Mainstream Bursary Programme 2027: For enquiries: Email: berlyne.rasool@adcorpgroup.com Tel: 086 010 6235 Submit your application online before the closing date. Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Important Notes Applications must be submitted via the official Sasol bursary portal Email applications will not be accepted Only South African citizens may apply

raw_data.description_full: Flyfofa Aviation Training invites South African youth to apply for the Advanced Pilot Training (300-Hour Building) Bursary Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Closing Date: 11 April 2026 Bursary Location: Gauteng Province, South Africa Overview of the Flyfofa Aviation NSF Bursary Programme 2026 Flyfofa Aviation Training, in partnership with the National Skills Fund (NSF) and the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET), is offering an exciting opportunity for aspiring pilots to advance their careers through the Advanced Pilot Training (300-Hour Building) Programme . This bursary programme is designed to help licensed pilots overcome one of the biggest barriers in aviation — building sufficient flight hours required for employment. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); If you already hold a Private Pilot Licence (PPL) or Commercial Pilot Licence (CPL) , this programme can help you transition from training into a professional aviation career. Bursary Programme Details Programme: Advanced Pilot Training (300 Flight Hours) Training Provider: Flyfofa Aviation Training Funding Partner: National Skills Fund (NSF) Location: Gauteng Province Application Code: PHB300 Minimum Qualification: PPL or CPL (SACAA recognised) Age Requirement: 35 years or younger Target Group: Previously disadvantaged individuals Closing Date: 11 April 2026 Why This Programme Matters Many pilots struggle to find employment after obtaining their licence due to insufficient flight hours . This programme directly addresses that challenge by offering: Structured flight hour accumulation Professional aviation exposure SACAA-aligned training progression Improved employability Completing an additional 300 flight hours significantly increases your chances of entering: Charter aviation companies Entry-level airline roles Flight instruction programmes Specialised aviation services Who Should Apply? This bursary is ideal for candidates who: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Already hold a PPL or CPL Need additional flight hours Cannot afford further training costs Want to enter commercial aviation Are committed to a long-term aviation career Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be a South African citizen Must be 35 years old or younger Must reside in Gauteng Province Must hold: Private Pilot Licence (PPL), or Commercial Pilot Licence (CPL) Must belong to previously disadvantaged groups (priority target) Documents Required Applicants must prepare: Copy of South African ID Cover letter (motivation) Proof of residence in Gauteng Copy of SACAA licence Last three copies of flight logbook Ensure all documents are clear and certified where required. How to Apply Online Application Click here to apply for Flyfofa Aviation NSF Bursary Programme – Advanced Pilot Training 2026 Email Application Alternatively, submit your application via email: Email: applications@flyfofa.co.za (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Subject Line: PHB300 Application – Advanced Hour Building Programme Attach the following: ID copy Cover letter Proof of residence SACAA licence copy Last three logbook copies This bursary programme is a rare opportunity for aspiring pilots to gain critical flight hours, reduce financial barriers, and move closer to a professional aviation career in South Africa.

raw_data.description_full: Bursaries are one of the best ways to fund your studies in South Africa, yet many students don’t fully understand how they work. While most people think bursaries are simply “free money for school,” there’s much more to them than meets the eye. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); If you’re planning to study at a university or TVET college, here are 5 things you probably didn’t know about bursaries that could change how you apply. 1. Some Bursaries Require You to Work After Graduation Many bursaries in South Africa come with a “work-back” obligation . This means that after completing your studies, you must work for the company or organisation that funded you. For example: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); If your bursary covered 3 years of study, you may be required to work for that organisation for 3 years. This is common in fields like engineering, teaching, healthcare, and government sectors. While this may sound restrictive, it actually helps you: Gain guaranteed work experience Avoid job hunting stress after graduating 2. Bursaries Don’t Always Cover Everything Not all bursaries are “full bursaries.” Some only cover certain expenses, such as: Tuition fees only Tuition + books Partial accommodation or transport It’s important to read the details carefully. A bursary might sound generous but still leave you responsible for: Food Accommodation Personal expenses Always check what is included before applying. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); 3. You Can Lose a Bursary If Your Marks Drop Getting a bursary is not a once-off achievement—you must maintain it . Most bursaries require: A minimum average (e.g. 60% or 65%) Passing all modules Good academic progress If your performance drops: Your bursary can be suspended or cancelled You may even be required to repay funds in some cases Consistency matters just as much as getting accepted. 4. Some Bursaries Are Only for Specific Fields Bursaries are often designed to support skills shortages in South Africa. This means they are targeted toward specific careers, such as: Engineering Accounting Medicine and Nursing Teaching (especially Maths and Science) IT and Data Science If you’re studying a general degree, you may find fewer bursary opportunities compared to high-demand fields. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Tip: Choosing a scarce-skill field can increase your chances of getting funded. 5. You Can Apply for More Than One Bursary Many students think they can only apply for one bursary—but that’s not true. You can apply for: Multiple bursaries at the same time Both private and government bursaries However: You usually cannot accept more than one full bursary at the same time If accepted for multiple, you must choose one Applying to more opportunities increases your chances of success. Final Thoughts Bursaries are more than just financial aid—they are opportunities that come with responsibilities, expectations, and long-term benefits. Understanding how they work can help you: Make better study choices Avoid common mistakes Increase your chances of securing funding If you’re serious about your future, start researching bursaries early, apply widely, and always read the requirements carefully. Bonus Tip Start applying as early as Grade 11 or early Grade 12 , as many bursaries close before final exams. The earlier you prepare, the better your chances. Click here to access bursaries.

raw_data.company: Moses Kotane Research Institute Digital Centre Facilitator
raw_data.description_full: Moses Kotane Research Institute invites unemployed South African graduates to apply for the Digital Centre Facilitator Experiential Learner Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: 15 May 2026 Learnership Location: KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa Moses Kotane Research Institute Digital Centre Facilitator Programme 2026 The Moses Kotane Research Institute (MKRI) is offering Experiential Learner opportunities for Digital Centre Facilitators across various digital centres in KwaZulu-Natal. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This programme is designed to provide practical ICT workplace experience to graduates with qualifications in Information Technology and related fields. Successful candidates will receive a monthly stipend of R7 000 for the duration of the 24-month programme. Programme Details Position: Experiential Learner: Digital Centre Facilitators Number of Posts: 5 Reference Number: EL/DCF5-01/2026 Duration: 24 Months Monthly Stipend: R7 000 Digital Centre Locations Successful applicants may be placed at the following centres: Inkosi Iangalibalele Digital Centre Edumbe Digital Centre Umkhuze Digital Centre Kokstad Digital Centre Impofana Digital Centre Applicants are encouraged to apply through their nearest MKRI Digital Centre. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: NQF Level 7 qualification in: Information Technology Or related field 0–2 years ICT facilitation experience Added Advantage Valid driver’s licence This opportunity is suitable for recent graduates and entry-level ICT candidates. Required Skills and Competencies Candidates should demonstrate knowledge in: Microsoft Office Suite Word processing Spreadsheet applications Presentation software Computer hardware and software maintenance Network troubleshooting Database maintenance Programming languages such as Java Electronics maintenance Office administration Applicants should also have: Good communication skills Strong interpersonal skills Ability to work with communities and learners Duties and Responsibilities Successful candidates will assist with: Facilitating ICT training at Digital Centres Monitoring learner attendance and performance Supporting learner development programmes Performing administration and reporting duties Assisting Digital Centre Supervisors Supporting digital literacy initiatives within communities Required Documents Applicants must submit: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Detailed CV Certified copy of South African ID Certified copy of driver’s licence (if applicable) Certified copies of qualifications Certified documents must not be older than 6 months. Important Disqualifiers Applicants may be disqualified for: Pending criminal cases Criminal convictions Pending disciplinary matters Submission of falsified documents Candidates are encouraged to provide truthful and accurate information. How to Apply Click here to apply for the Moses Kotane Research Institute Digital Centre Facilitator Programme 2026: Applicants should clearly indicate: Position title Reference Number: EL/DCF5-01/2026 Applications may also be submitted through the nearest MKRI Digital Centre. Contact Details Technical Enquiries Email: recruitment@moseskotane.com Why You Should Apply This programme provides valuable practical exposure in: ICT facilitation Digital literacy training Community technology support Network and hardware support Administration and reporting It is an excellent opportunity for graduates looking to gain workplace experience and strengthen their careers in the ICT and digital services sector.
