raw_data.description_full: Sasol invites South African students to apply for the Mainstream Bursary Programme for the 2027 academic year. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Bursary Application Opening Date: 01 April 2026 Bursary Application Closing Date: 17 May 2026 Study Location: Recognised South African Universities Bursary Overview Sasol is offering a highly sought-after Mainstream Bursary Programme for 2027 , aimed at supporting talented students pursuing careers in engineering and science fields . This bursary is designed to not only fund your studies but also develop your career through mentorship, training, and potential employment opportunities . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); What the Bursary Covers Full tuition fees Accommodation and study-related expenses Structured mentorship and support Possible placement in Sasol’s Graduate Development Programme Important: This bursary includes a workback/payback obligation after completing your studies. Fields of Study Undergraduate (BEng / BSc) You can apply if you plan to study: Chemical Engineering Civil Engineering Electrical Engineering Electronic Engineering Industrial Engineering Mechanical Engineering Mining Engineering Metallurgical Engineering Chemistry (up to Honours level) Geology (up to Honours level) Data Science (Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Operations Research) Undergraduate (BEng Tech) Mine Surveying (University of Technology only) Postgraduate (MSc / MEng / PhD) Fields include: Chemical Engineering (Fluid Dynamics, Thermodynamics, Process Modelling) Chemistry (Catalysis, Polymer Science, Environmental Science, Analytical Chemistry) Cyber Security Data Science (Applied Mathematics, Statistics) Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Be a South African citizen Be under 30 years of age Intend to study full-time at a recognised university Have strong academic performance Important Academic Requirements Mathematics (NOT Maths Literacy) is required Only degree programmes are accepted (no diplomas) Distance learning (e.g., UNISA) is not accepted Important Conditions Only one application per applicant is allowed Applications must be submitted online only Incomplete or incorrect applications will be disqualified Selection is highly competitive Awards are based on academic merit and Sasol business needs Required Documents Make sure you prepare the following: Latest academic results (Grade 11 or most recent) Certified copy of your ID Any additional supporting documents required How to Apply Click here to apply for Sasol Mainstream Bursary Programme 2027: For enquiries: Email: berlyne.rasool@adcorpgroup.com Tel: 086 010 6235 Important Notes Applications must be submitted through the official Sasol bursary portal Email applications will not be accepted Late applications will not be considered

raw_data.description_full: The Gauteng Department of Environment invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Internship Programme 2026 – 2028. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 22 May 2026 Internship Location: Johannesburg and Pretoria, Gauteng, South Africa The Gauteng Department of Environment is offering a 24-month non-renewable internship programme for unemployed graduates who hold National Diplomas, Bachelor’s Degrees, or Honour’s Degrees in various fields. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Gauteng Department of Environment Internship Programme 2026 The internship programme aims to provide structured workplace experience and practical training to graduates in different disciplines. Successful candidates will participate in projects, workplace activities, and professional development initiatives under the supervision of experienced officials. Available Internship Opportunities Air Quality Internship 2026 Reference Number: AQ2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Applicants must have a Diploma or Degree in: Environmental Science Environmental Management Chemistry Chemical Engineering Earth Science Natural Science Pollution & Waste Management Internship 2026 Reference Number: PWM2026 Number of Posts: 2 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Requirements Applicants must hold a Diploma or Degree in: Environmental Science Environmental Management Natural Science Geography Environmental & Impact Management Internship 2026 Reference Number: EIM2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Diploma or Degree in Environmental Science or Environmental Management Environmental Policy, Planning & Coordination Internship 2026 Reference Number: EPPC2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Pretoria (Council for Scientific & Industrial Research) Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Diploma or Degree in Environmental Science or Environmental Management Compliance & Enforcement Internship 2026 Reference Number: CE2026 Number of Posts: 4 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Applicants must have a Diploma or Degree in: Environmental Science Environmental Management Natural Science Office of the Head of Department Internship 2026 Reference Number: HOD2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Requirements Applicants must have a Diploma or Degree in: Public Management Public Administration Office & Technology Management Financial Management Internship 2026 Reference Number: FM2026 Number of Posts: 2 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements N6, Diploma, or Degree in Financial Management or Accounting Science Organisational Development Internship 2026 Reference Number: OD2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Applicants must have a Diploma or Degree in: Work-study Operations Management Management Services Production Management ICT Internship 2026 Reference Number: ICT2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Applicants must hold a Degree or Diploma in: Computer Science Information Technology Business Informatics Communications Internship 2026 Reference Number: COM2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Diploma or Degree in Media Studies or related qualification Graphic Design Internship 2026 Reference Number: GD2026 Number of Posts: 1 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Diploma or Degree in Graphic Design or related qualification Planning, Monitoring & Evaluation Internship 2026 Reference Number: ICT2026 Number of Posts: 2 Centre: Head Office, Johannesburg Monthly Stipend: R8 174.75 Requirements Applicants must have a Diploma or Degree in: Social Science Public Management & Governance Monitoring & Evaluation Internship Duties Successful interns will: Receive structured workplace exposure Participate in projects and departmental programmes Attend training and development activities Gain practical work experience related to their qualifications Work under the supervision of experienced professionals Important Requirements Applicants must be unemployed South African graduates Applicants must not have participated in a Public Service internship programme before Qualifications must be obtained from accredited institutions Only shortlisted candidates will submit certified copies of documents Applications must include: Fully completed new Z83 form Detailed CV Applications must be submitted as one PDF document JPEG or picture applications will not be accepted How to Apply Applications should be emailed to: Email Address: GDEnvInternship@gauteng.gov.za Applicants must quote the relevant reference number in the email subject line. Enquiries Contact Person: Mr. Livhuwani Mutswana Telephone: (011) 240 2590 Important Dates Details Information Closing Date 22 May 2026 Internship Duration 24 Months Monthly Stipend R8 174.75 Conclusion The Gauteng Department of Environment Internship Programme 2026 – 2028 offers graduates an excellent opportunity to gain practical government work experience in environmental management, ICT, finance, communications, and other fields. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply before the closing date.

raw_data.description_full: South African students and graduates still have time to apply for some of the best internship and graduate opportunities currently open across government departments, municipalities, healthcare institutions, universities, and private companies. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); These programmes offer valuable workplace experience, graduate development, and career growth opportunities in fields such as finance, healthcare, HR, administration, marketing, logistics, and engineering. Below are the top internship and graduate opportunities closing soon in May 2026. 1. Old Mutual Graduate Accelerated Programme 2026 Old Mutual Limited invites graduates to apply for the Graduate Accelerated Programme (GAP) 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Closing Date: 10 May 2026 Location: South Africa Apply Here: 2. Nyandeni Local Municipality Internships 2026 Nyandeni Local Municipality is offering internship opportunities in building maintenance and technical services. Closing Date: 11 May 2026 at 15:30 Location: Libode & Ngqeleni, Eastern Cape Apply Here: 3. Northlink College WIL Internships 2026 Northlink TVET College invites NCV Level 4 graduates to apply for WIL opportunities. Closing Date: 11 May 2026 at 12:00 PM Location: Western Cape Apply Here: 4. Lancet Laboratories Pre-Analytical Internships 2026 Lancet Laboratories is offering internship opportunities within the healthcare and pathology sector. Closing Date: 12 May 2026 Location: South Africa Apply Here : 5. Joburg City Theatres Marketing Internships 2026 Joburg City Theatres invites creative graduates to apply for marketing internships. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Closing Date: 12 May 2026 Location: Johannesburg, Gauteng Apply Here: 6. RAF Graduate Internships 2026 Road Accident Fund invites unemployed graduates to apply for its graduate internship programme. Closing Date: 12 May 2026 Location: Johannesburg, Gauteng Apply Here: 7. Western Cape Department of Health Graduate Internships 2026 Western Cape Department of Health and Wellness is offering graduate internship opportunities in various fields. Closing Date: 13 May 2026 Location: Western Cape Apply Here: 8. George Municipality Financial Management Internships 2026 George Local Municipality invites finance graduates to apply for its internship programme. Closing Date: 14 May 2026 at 16:30 Location: George, Western Cape Apply Here: 9. UWC W&RSETA Administrative Internships 2026 University of the Western Cape, together with W&RSETA, is offering administrative internship opportunities. Closing Date: 15 May 2026 at 17:00 Location: Western Cape Apply Here: 10. Council for Geoscience HR Internships 2026 Council for Geoscience is recruiting Human Resources interns. Closing Date: 15 May 2026 Location: Pretoria, Gauteng Apply Here: Graduates are encouraged to apply early and ensure all supporting documents are ready before deadlines close. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Final Thoughts Internships and graduate programmes are among the best ways for unemployed graduates to gain practical work experience and improve their chances of long-term employment. If you qualify for any of these programmes, apply as soon as possible before the closing dates pass.

raw_data.description_full: PwC invites South African graduates to apply for the Graduate Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Graduate Programme Application Closing Date: 07 June 2026 Graduate Programme Location: South Africa About the PwC Graduate Programme PwC offers a structured three-year Graduate Programme designed to provide graduates with practical workplace experience, mentorship, and professional development opportunities. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Graduates will gain exposure to real business projects across industries such as: Banking Insurance Employee Benefits Analytics Risk and Compliance Technology and Consulting Participants will work in a professional and supportive environment while building valuable industry skills and international exposure. Available Graduate Opportunities SAP: Digital Trust Graduate Programme Applicants must be pursuing an Honours degree in: Computer Science BCom (IT) Accounting IT (BSc or BCom) Management Accounting Internal Auditing Business Science Risk Management Engineering Tax Programme (SAIT) Applicants must currently be studying: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); CTA PGDA Honours in Accounting Transfer Pricing Programme (SAIT) Applicants must hold or be completing a postgraduate qualification in: Tax Accounting Law Requirement: Minimum 60% average. Foundation for the Future (FftF) Consulting Graduate Programme Applicants should hold a degree in: Business Economics Econometrics Engineering Geoinformatics Industrial Psychology Mathematics Statistics Supply Chain & Logistics Technology Data & AI Information Systems Internal Audit (GRIA) Graduate Programme Applicants pursuing an Honours degree in: Internal Audit Information Systems Data Analysis-related fields SAP Centre of Excellence (COE) Graduate Programme 2027 Requirements: Any degree with strong IT or systems component Honours degree advantageous SAP experience beneficial but not required Digital Trust (IT Audit) Graduate Programme Open to students pursuing Honours degrees in: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Information Technology Computer Science AI-related IT programmes Engineering Mathematics Internal Auditing Business Science Risk Management Economics Accounting Management Accounting Forensics Graduate Programme Applicants must hold qualifications in: BCom Forensic Accounting (Honours) BCom Accounting (Non-CA route) Internal Audit Forensic Science and Technology Law (LLB or BCom Law) Criminology Risk Management Economics/Econometrics What You Will Gain Successful graduates will receive: Hands-on business experience Professional mentorship Exposure to multiple industries International career opportunities Technical and leadership development Real project involvement from the start How to Apply NB. Use the given links to Apply SAP: Digital Trust Graduate Programme Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the SAP: Digital Trust Graduate Programme Tax Programme (SAIT) Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the Tax Programme (SAIT) Transfer Pricing Programme (SAIT) Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the Transfer Pricing Programme (SAIT) Foundation for the Future (FftF) – Consulting Graduate Programme Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the Foundation for the Future (FftF) – Consulting Graduate Programme Internal Audit (GRIA) Graduate Programme Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the Internal Audit (GRIA) Graduate Programme SAP Centre of Excellence (COE) Graduate Programme 2027 Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the SAP Centre of Excellence (COE) 2027 Digital Trust (IT Audit) Graduate Programme Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the Digital Trust (IT Audit) Graduate Programme Forensics Graduate Programme Closing date: 07 June 2026 Apply Online for the Forensics Graduate Programme ​​​​​​ This graduate programme is ideal for ambitious graduates who want to build careers in consulting, technology, finance, audit, risk, tax, and digital transformation while working for one of the world’s leading professional services firms.

raw_data.description_full: Prince Albert Municipality invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Financial Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 15 May 2026 at 15:00 Internship Location: Prince Albert Municipality, Western Cape, South Africa Prince Albert Municipality Financial Internship 2026 Prince Albert Municipality is offering a Financial Internship opportunity within the Directorate: Financial Services. The internship programme aims to provide practical training and workplace experience in municipal finance management. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The programme is aligned with the Municipal Finance Management Internship Programme (MFMIP), which focuses on building financial management skills in local government. Internship Details Position: Financial Intern (X1) Directorate: Financial Services Post Identification: WC052-1.2.3.1.5 Contract Duration: Two-year fixed-term contract Remuneration: R108 000 per annum (all-inclusive) Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: A three-year Bachelor’s Degree or National Diploma with majors in: Accounting Economics Finance Risk Management Auditing or related fields Computer literacy (MS Office) Good management and interpersonal skills Good communication skills Ability to work under pressure and pay attention to detail Effective communication skills in at least two of the three official languages of the Western Cape Applicants must be between 21 and 35 years old Overview of the Internship Programme The Municipal Finance Management Internship Programme (MFMIP) is a structured professional training and work experience programme. The internship provides exposure to: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Municipal budgeting Treasury functions Financial administration Policy implementation Municipal finance systems Interns will sign both an employment contract and an internship agreement. Required Documents Applicants must submit: Completed prescribed application form Comprehensive CV Certified copies of qualifications Academic transcript Certified copy of ID document Supporting documents All certified copies must be attached to the application. How to Apply Click here to apply for Prince Albert Municipality Financial Internship 2026: Applications can be submitted via email to: jobapplications@pamun.gov.za Or posted/delivered to: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Mr Abridon Sass – Human Resource Management Department 33 Church Street Prince Albert 6930 Enquiries Job-related enquiries: Mr David-Leigh Willemse Tel: 023 541 1668 Email: willemsed@pamun.gov.za HR enquiries: Mr Abridon Sass Tel: 023 541 8108 Email: abridon@pamun.gov.za Important Notes Late applications will not be accepted. Incomplete applications will be disqualified. Applicants may be subjected to interviews, practical tests, and verification checks. Prince Albert Municipality is an equal opportunity employer. Preference may be given in line with Employment Equity objectives. If applicants do not receive feedback within eight weeks after the closing date, they should consider their application unsuccessful. Prince Albert Municipality Communications Intern 2026 Meta Description: Apply for the Prince Albert Municipality Communications Intern 2026. Salary R100 000 per annum. Closing date 15 May 2026. Prince Albert Municipality invites South African graduates to apply for the Communications Internship Programme 2026. Internship Application Closing Date: 15 May 2026 at 15:00 Internship Location: Prince Albert Municipality, Western Cape, South Africa Prince Albert Municipality Communications Internship 2026 Prince Albert Municipality is recruiting a Communications Intern within the Office of the Municipal Manager. The internship is suitable for graduates interested in communications, journalism, media relations, marketing, and digital communication. Internship Details Position: Communications Intern Directorate: Office of the Municipal Manager Contract Duration: One-year fixed-term contract Salary: R100 000 per annum (all-inclusive) Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: National Diploma or Degree in: Public Relations Communications Journalism Marketing or related qualification Computer literacy (MS Office) Good communication and interpersonal skills Ability to work under pressure Knowledge of social media and digital communication tools Good organisational skills Ability to work overtime when required Graduates with no prior work experience are encouraged to apply. Key Responsibilities Successful candidates will assist with: Communication and marketing programmes Municipal social media management Website content updates Drafting newsletters and media releases Public participation initiatives Organising municipal events and campaigns Administrative support for the Communications Unit Public enquiries and correspondence Required Documents Applicants must submit: Prescribed application form Comprehensive CV Certified copies of qualifications Certified ID copy Supporting documents How to Apply Applications can be emailed to: jobapplications@pamun.gov.za Or delivered to: Mr Abridon Sass – Human Resource Management Department 33 Church Street Prince Albert 6930 Enquiries Job-related enquiries: Mr Matthys Giliomee Tel: 023 541 1036 Email: thys@pamun.gov.za HR enquiries: Mr Abridon Sass Tel: 023 541 1036 Email: abridon@pamun.gov.za Important Notes Late applications will not be accepted. Candidates may be subjected to interviews and assessments. Non-submission of academic records may lead to disqualification. Prince Albert Municipality supports Employment Equity and encourages persons with disabilities and women to apply. If no response is received within 90 days after the closing date, applicants should regard their applications as unsuccessful.

raw_data.description_full: Funda Wande invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Internship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 18 May 2026 Internship Location: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa Funda Wande Internship Programme 2026 Funda Wande is offering exciting internship opportunities for young South Africans who are passionate about education, communication, media, administration, and digital learning. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The internship programme provides practical workplace experience through hands-on involvement in administration, communication support, media production, research, content management, and project coordination. Successful candidates will gain valuable professional experience within a purpose-driven educational organisation. Available Internship Opportunities HR Intern Applicants must have a: Diploma or Degree in: Human Resources Management Industrial Psychology Related field Office Reception Intern (In-Office Role) Applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Relevant qualification in: Office Management Business Management E-Learning Assistant Intern Applicants must have: Minimum qualification of Matric or Bachelor’s degree in: Communications Media Studies Digital Marketing Related field Communications Assistant Intern Applicants must have: Grade 12/Matric or Bachelor’s degree in: Communications Media Studies Digital Marketing Related field Be a South African citizen Be between 18 and 34 years old Never held a full-time position before Preferably not participated in a previous internship or learnership Media Assistant Intern Applicants must have: Grade 12/Matric or Bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification Be a South African citizen Be between 18 and 34 years old Never held a full-time job before Preferably not participated in a previous internship or learnership Skills and Competencies Successful applicants should demonstrate: Good communication skills Computer literacy Attention to detail Teamwork Willingness to learn Organisational skills Passion for education and social impact What Interns Will Gain Interns will receive: Practical workplace experience Exposure to project coordination and administration Experience in communication and digital media support Opportunity to build professional skills Mentorship and development opportunities Exposure to the education and non-profit sector Why You Should Apply The Funda Wande Internship Programme is an excellent opportunity for graduates seeking workplace exposure in a meaningful environment focused on education and social development. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The programme allows interns to build valuable experience while contributing to impactful educational initiatives across South Africa. How to Apply HR Intern Closing date: 18 May 2026 Apply Online for the HR Internship Office Reception Intern (In-Office Role) Closing date: 14 May 2026 Apply Online for the Office Reception Internship (In-Office Role) E-Learning Assistant Intern Closing date: 14 May 2026 Apply Online for the E-Learning Assistant Internship Communications Assistant Intern Closing date: 14 May 2026 Apply Online for the Communications Assistant Internship Media Assistant Intern Closing date: 14 May 2026 Apply Online for the Media Assistant Internship Important Notes Preference may be given to candidates who have not previously participated in internships or learnerships. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Applicants are encouraged to apply before the closing date. Funda Wande encourages motivated and passionate young South Africans to apply.

raw_data.description_full: Sasol invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the Artisan Chemical Plant Operator Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: 08 May 2026 Learnership Location: Secunda and Sasolburg, South Africa About the Sasol Learnership Programme Sasol is a leading global integrated chemicals and energy company with over 75 years of experience. The company is committed to developing young talent and providing structured career development opportunities. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This 36-month fixed-term learnership combines both theoretical training and practical workplace experience, giving learners the opportunity to gain valuable skills in the chemical and energy sector. Learnership Programme Details Position: Artisan Chemical Plant Operator Reference Number: 12032 Duration: 36 Months (Fixed Term Contract) Department: HR Operations (PSR: HR Operations) Programme Type: Learnership (Workplace + Institutional Learning) Minimum Requirements To qualify, applicants must meet one of the following: Option 1: Grade 12 (National Senior Certificate) with: Mathematics / Technical Mathematics English Physical Science / Technical Science OR Option 2: N3 Certificate with: Mathematics English Engineering Science Key Responsibilities Successful candidates will: Participate fully in the learnership programme Complete all required assessments Take part in workplace readiness activities Gain practical experience in plant operations Follow safety and operational procedures in a chemical environment Why Apply for Sasol Learnerships? Gain industry-relevant skills in chemical plant operations Work with one of South Africa’s top energy companies Improve your employability in engineering and manufacturing sectors Receive structured training and mentorship How to Apply Click here to apply for Sasol Artisan Chemical Plant Operator Learnerships 2026: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Important: Ensure you apply before the closing date (08 May 2026) Prepare certified copies of your qualifications and ID Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted If you are passionate about engineering, chemical processes, and building a career in the energy sector, this Sasol Learnership Programme is a great opportunity to start your journey.

raw_data.company: South West Gauteng TVET College WIL
raw_data.description_full: South West Gauteng TVET College invites South African unemployed graduates to apply for the Work Integrated Learning (WIL) Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Internship Application Closing Date: 22 May 2026 Internship Location: South West Gauteng TVET College, Florida Campus, Roodepoort, Gauteng, South Africa About the WIL Programme South West Gauteng TVET College (SWGC), in partnership with SETAs including MICT SETA, W&R SETA, and FoodBev SETA, is offering Work Integrated Learning (WIL) opportunities to students who have completed their N6 qualification . (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); This programme allows students to gain 18 months of workplace experience , which is required to obtain their National N Diploma . Programme Details Programme: Work Integrated Learning (WIL) Duration: 18 Months Monthly Stipend: R3 500 Start Date: Expected 01 June 2026 Fields of Study Opportunities are available in the following fields: Management Assistant Business Management Financial Management Marketing Management Human Resource Management Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Have completed an N6 qualification in a relevant field Require 18 months of WIL to obtain a National N Diploma Be unemployed and available for the full duration Provide a letter from a host employer (if applicable) Required Documents Applicants must submit certified copies of the following: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Comprehensive CV Matric Certificate or NC(V) Level 4 Certificate Certified copy of ID College confirmation letter (confirming need for 18 months WIL) Proof of residence Bank confirmation letter Affidavit confirming you are not part of any SETA-funded programme SARS registration letter N4–N6 certificates Note: All documents must be clearly certified. How to Apply Click here to apply for South West Gauteng TVET College WIL Programme 2026: Applications must be submitted via hand delivery to the following address: South West Gauteng TVET College Florida Campus 40/42 Goldman Street Florida, Roodepoort Enquiries: Contact Person: Ms Hitekani Mkansi Email: nbdu1@swgc.co.za Important Notes Incomplete applications will not be considered Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted If you do not receive feedback within 15 days after closing, consider your application unsuccessful This WIL programme is an excellent opportunity for N6 graduates to gain practical workplace experience , complete their qualifications, and improve their employability in the business and management fields. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

raw_data.description_full: OUTsurance invites South African unemployed youth to apply for the OSS Hastings Learnership Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Learnership Application Closing Date: Not specified (Apply ASAP) Learnership Location: Centurion, Gauteng, South Africa About the OUTsurance Learnership Programme The OSS Hastings Learnership is an 18-month structured work-based programme aimed at providing unemployed youth with practical workplace experience in the insurance and financial services industry. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The programme focuses on developing call centre and customer service skills while offering participants the opportunity to complete formal training and gain valuable industry exposure. Successful learners will sign a fixed-term learnership agreement and receive a monthly stipend. Learnership Details Programme: OSS Hastings Learnership Duration: 18 Months Monthly Stipend: R9 000 Industry: Insurance / Financial Services Programme Type: INSETA Learnership Working Hours and Shift Requirements Participants will work according to a roster system, including: Friday to Monday shifts (9 hours) Saturdays (8 hours) Sundays (5 hours) Two weekdays off (Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday) One weekday dedicated to on-site training Public Holidays Learners may be required to work on South African public holidays if scheduled Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Matric / Grade 12 Basic computer literacy (MS Office) No work experience required To qualify for the INSETA Learnership: Must be a South African citizen Must be between 18 and 35 years old Must not have previously participated in an INSETA Learnership Must have been unemployed for the past 6 months Skills and Competencies Successful candidates should demonstrate: Strong interpersonal skills Ability to build relationships Professional communication skills Ability to multitask and work under pressure Adaptability and positive attitude Self-motivation and willingness to learn Why Apply for This Learnership? Gain valuable call centre and insurance industry experience Earn a monthly stipend of R9 000 Receive formal training and workplace exposure Improve your employability in the financial services sector Build customer service and communication skills How to Apply Click here to apply for OUTsurance OSS Hastings Learnership 2026: This learnership is a great opportunity for young South Africans who want to start a career in the insurance, customer service, and financial services industry while gaining practical workplace experience.

raw_data.description_full: Woodlands Dairy invites South African youth to apply for the Millwright Apprentice Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Apprenticeship Application Closing Date: 22 May 2026 Apprenticeship Location: Humansdorp, Eastern Cape, South Africa Woodlands Dairy Millwright Apprentice Programme 2026 Woodlands Dairy is offering a structured Millwright Apprenticeship Programme aimed at developing aspiring technicians into qualified trade-tested Millwrights. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The programme combines formal technical training, practical workplace experience, and mentorship in both mechanical and electrical engineering disciplines, aligned with FoodBev SETA requirements. This apprenticeship is ideal for candidates seeking long-term technical careers within the FMCG and industrial engineering environment. Apprenticeship Details Position: Millwright Apprentice – 2026 Division: Operations Business Unit: Maintenance Services Industry: FMCG Contract Duration: 36 Months Employment Type: Apprenticeship Key Performance Areas Successful apprentices will: Participate in a registered Millwright Apprenticeship Programme Complete required theoretical and practical training Gain exposure to: Mechanical maintenance Electrical maintenance Equipment installation Complete FoodBev SETA assessments and requirements Adhere to workplace health and safety standards Demonstrate commitment to continuous learning and technical development Progress toward trade testing and certification Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Technical Grade 12 OR N3 qualification Qualification in: Electrical Engineering Mechanical Engineering Related technical field Strong interest in becoming a Millwright Willingness to participate in structured apprenticeship training Skills and Competencies Ideal candidates should demonstrate: Technical aptitude Good work ethic Reliability Discipline Willingness to learn Commitment to skills development Interest in mechanical and electrical systems What You Will Gain Successful candidates will receive: Formal apprenticeship training Practical workplace experience Exposure to industrial engineering systems Mentorship from experienced technicians Opportunity to become a qualified trade-tested Millwright Why You Should Apply Millwrights remain highly sought-after artisans across industries such as: Manufacturing FMCG Food processing Mining Engineering Industrial maintenance This apprenticeship provides an excellent pathway into a long-term technical career while allowing apprentices to develop both electrical and mechanical skills. How to Apply Apply Online Important Notes The apprenticeship programme runs for 36 months. Apprentices must complete both practical and theoretical training requirements. Successful candidates will work toward becoming trade-tested Millwrights. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. Candidates interested in technical and engineering careers are encouraged to apply before the closing date. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

raw_data.description_full: Motus Corporation invites South African youth to apply for the Apprentice Level Year 1 Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Apprenticeship Application Closing Date: 12 May 2026 Apprenticeship Location: Edenvale, Gauteng, South Africa Motus Toyota Bedfordview Apprentice Programme 2026 Motus Toyota Bedfordview is offering an exciting Level 1 Apprenticeship opportunity for aspiring Motor Mechanic Technicians. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The programme is designed for individuals passionate about vehicle repairs and automotive engineering who want to gain practical training and technical skills within the automotive industry. Successful candidates will undergo an apprenticeship training programme for up to three years while working under the supervision of experienced technicians. Apprenticeship Details Position: Apprentice Level Year 1 Division: Retail Business Unit: Toyota & Hino Industry: Automotive Location: Edenvale, Gauteng Employment Type: Contract / Apprenticeship Key Responsibilities Successful apprentices will: Assist technicians with vehicle repairs Remove and replace vehicle parts and components Jack up vehicles and place trestles safely Assist with oil changes and lubrication under supervision Remove and replace wheels, batteries, filters, and other components Attend prescribed technical and level training courses Assist with workshop housekeeping Maintain tools and equipment Follow safety procedures and workshop standards Minimum Requirements Applicants must have: (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Grade 12 with: Pure Mathematics Physical Science Clear criminal record Added Advantages Valid driver’s licence Technical skills or automotive knowledge Skills and Competencies Ideal candidates should demonstrate: Interest in motor vehicle repairs Technical aptitude Teamwork skills Good customer service orientation Willingness to learn Strong work ethic Attention to detail What You Will Gain Successful apprentices will receive: Practical automotive workshop experience Technical training and development Exposure to Toyota and Hino vehicle systems Opportunity to develop into a qualified Motor Mechanic Technician Hands-on industry experience within a leading automotive company Why You Should Apply The automotive industry continues to offer strong artisan career opportunities for technically skilled individuals. This apprenticeship provides young South Africans with an opportunity to: Gain valuable technical experience Learn from experienced technicians Build a long-term automotive career Develop practical workshop skills How to Apply Apply Online Important Notes Apprenticeship training may run for a maximum of three years. Candidates must be willing to attend technical training courses. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. Applicants interested in becoming qualified Motor Mechanic Technicians are encouraged to apply before the closing date. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

raw_data.description_full: Toyota Boshoku South Africa invites unemployed South African youth to apply for the Millwright Apprenticeship Programme 2026. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Apprenticeship Application Closing Date: 29 May 2026 Apprenticeship Location: Amanzimtoti, Durban, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa Toyota Boshoku South Africa Millwright Apprenticeship Programme 2026 Toyota Boshoku South Africa (TBSA) is offering a 48-month apprenticeship programme aimed at training future qualified Millwright artisans. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); The programme combines structured technical training, practical workplace exposure, and a Trade Test qualification process that will prepare successful candidates for careers in the engineering and manufacturing industry. Apprenticeship Details Programme: Millwright Apprenticeship Programme 2026 Number of Positions: 5 Duration: 48 Months Trade: Millwright Industry: Automotive Manufacturing Minimum Requirements Applicants must: Be South African citizens Have passed Matric with: Mathematics Physical Science OR Technical Matric Achieve a minimum of 50% Have completed: N2 Electrical with all four modules passed including Trade Theory N2 Mechanical with all four modules passed Required Documents Applicants must submit: Updated CV in PDF format Certified copy of South African ID Certified copies of qualifications Incomplete applications may not be considered. (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Skills and Competencies Successful candidates should demonstrate: Good written and verbal communication skills Strong work ethic Initiative and self-motivation Teamwork skills Ability to build relationships across departments What the Programme Offers The apprenticeship programme provides: Structured technical training Practical workplace experience Artisan development Trade Test preparation Opportunity to qualify as a certified Millwright artisan Why You Should Apply Millwrights are highly skilled artisans responsible for maintaining and repairing industrial machinery within manufacturing and production environments. This apprenticeship provides an excellent opportunity for technically inclined youth interested in: Mechanical systems Electrical systems Industrial maintenance Manufacturing engineering Automotive production environments How to Apply Applications must be emailed to: TBSA.Recruitment@toyota-boshoku.com Or apply via LinkedIn: Toyota Boshoku South Africa LinkedIn (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({}); Email Subject Line Use the subject line: Apprenticeship Important Notes No late applications will be accepted. Employment Equity objectives will be considered during the recruitment process. Applicants must provide complete and accurate information for shortlisting purposes. The positions are advertised internally and externally. Young South Africans interested in becoming qualified artisans are encouraged to apply before the closing date.
